headline: Models split on SPY direction while XOM surges 20% on oil tensions
summary: A day of mixed signals saw models disagreeing on broader market direction, with SPY predictions split 4-2 bullish despite oil hitting $126. Energy plays dominated with XOM rocketing past all targets, while tech names showed surprising strength despite inflation concerns. Volatility predictions completely missed as VIX dropped despite geopolitical tensions.

consensus_picks.note: Four models correctly called SPY's rise to 718.66 despite Claude's bearish oil-driven concerns.
Three models correctly predicted Apple's gain to 271.35, with only Gemini calling for a decline.
Both models wrongly predicted NVDA would rise, missing its drop to 199.57.
Tech-heavy QQQ rose as predicted by both models, closing near their targets at 667.74.

best_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence XOM call captured a massive 20% surge to 154.33 on Iran blockade tensions.

worst_call.summary: Gemini's bearish AAPL call targeting 185.50 completely missed as the stock soared to 271.35.
